Package in.co.rays.proj4.model

Class TimetableModel

java.lang.Object
in.co.rays.proj4.model.TimetableModel
public class TimetableModel extends Object
TimetableModel is a Model class that handles all database operations for the Timetable entity. It is used to perform CRUD operations on the ST_TIMETABLE table. This class contains methods for adding, updating, deleting, searching, and retrieving timetable records, along with duplicate-check methods based on course, subject, semester, exam date, and exam time. It interacts with the database using JDBC and communicates with the TimetableBean for data transfer.
Author:
Harshit Panchal

  • Constructor Details

    • TimetableModel

      public TimetableModel()

  • Method Details

    • nextpk

      public Integer nextpk() throws DataBaseException
      Returns the next available primary key for the ST_TIMETABLE table by fetching the current maximum ID and incrementing it by 1.
      Returns:
      next available primary key as Integer
      Throws:
      DataBaseException - if any database error occurs while fetching the key

    • add

      public long add(TimetableBean bean) throws Exception
      Adds a new Timetable record to the ST_TIMETABLE table. Before inserting, it fetches the associated course and subject details using their respective model classes. Rolls back the transaction if any error occurs during insertion.
      Parameters:
      bean - the TimetableBean containing the timetable details to be added
      Returns:
      the primary key of the newly added timetable record
      Throws:
      Exception - if any error occurs during the add operation or rollback

    • update

      public void update(TimetableBean bean) throws ApplicationException
      Updates an existing Timetable record in the ST_TIMETABLE table. Before updating, it fetches the latest course and subject details using their respective model classes. Rolls back the transaction if any error occurs during the update.
      Parameters:
      bean - the TimetableBean containing the updated timetable details
      Throws:
      ApplicationException - if any error occurs during the update operation or rollback

    • delete

      public void delete(TimetableBean bean) throws ApplicationException
      Deletes an existing Timetable record from the ST_TIMETABLE table based on the ID present in the provided TimetableBean. Rolls back the transaction if any error occurs during deletion.
      Parameters:
      bean - the TimetableBean containing the ID of the timetable to be deleted
      Throws:
      ApplicationException - if any error occurs during the delete operation or rollback

    • findBYpk

      public TimetableBean findBYpk(long pk) throws ApplicationException
      Finds and returns a Timetable record from the ST_TIMETABLE table based on the given primary key.
      Parameters:
      pk - the primary key of the timetable record to be fetched
      Returns:
      TimetableBean containing the timetable details, or null if not found
      Throws:
      ApplicationException - if any error occurs while fetching the record

    • checkByCourseName

      public TimetableBean checkByCourseName(Long courseId, Date examDate) throws ApplicationException
      Checks and returns a Timetable record from the ST_TIMETABLE table by matching the given course ID and exam date. Used to detect duplicate timetable entries for the same course on the same exam date.
      Parameters:
      courseId - the ID of the course to check against
      examDate - the exam date to check against
      Returns:
      TimetableBean if a matching record is found, or null if not found
      Throws:
      ApplicationException - if any error occurs while fetching the record

    • checkBySubjectName

      public TimetableBean checkBySubjectName(Long courseId, Long subjectId, Date examDate) throws ApplicationException
      Checks and returns a Timetable record from the ST_TIMETABLE table by matching the given course ID, subject ID, and exam date. Used to detect duplicate timetable entries for the same course and subject on the same exam date.
      Parameters:
      courseId - the ID of the course to check against
      subjectId - the ID of the subject to check against
      examDate - the exam date to check against
      Returns:
      TimetableBean if a matching record is found, or null if not found
      Throws:
      ApplicationException - if any error occurs while fetching the record

    • checkBySemester

      public TimetableBean checkBySemester(Long courseId, Long subjectId, String semester, Date examDate) throws ApplicationException
      Checks and returns a Timetable record from the ST_TIMETABLE table by matching the given course ID, subject ID, semester, and exam date. Used to detect duplicate timetable entries for the same course, subject, and semester on the same exam date.
      Parameters:
      courseId - the ID of the course to check against
      subjectId - the ID of the subject to check against
      semester - the semester to check against
      examDate - the exam date to check against
      Returns:
      TimetableBean if a matching record is found, or null if not found
      Throws:
      ApplicationException - if any error occurs while fetching the record

    • checkByExamTime

      public TimetableBean checkByExamTime(Long courseId, Long subjectId, String semester, Date examDate, String examTime, String description) throws ApplicationException
      Checks and returns a Timetable record from the ST_TIMETABLE table by matching the given course ID, subject ID, semester, exam date, exam time, and description. Used to detect fully duplicate timetable entries across all key scheduling fields.
      Parameters:
      courseId - the ID of the course to check against
      subjectId - the ID of the subject to check against
      semester - the semester to check against
      examDate - the exam date to check against
      examTime - the exam time to check against
      description - the description to check against
      Returns:
      TimetableBean if a matching record is found, or null if not found
      Throws:
      ApplicationException - if any error occurs while fetching the record

    • search

      public List<TimetableBean> search(TimetableBean bean, int pageNo, int pageSize) throws ApplicationException
      Searches for Timetable records in the ST_TIMETABLE table based on the provided search criteria with optional pagination support. If the bean is null, all records are returned. Supports filtering by id, course ID, course name, subject ID, subject name, semester, description, exam date, and exam time.
      Parameters:
      bean - the TimetableBean containing the search criteria, or null to fetch all records
      pageNo - the page number for pagination (use 0 for no pagination)
      pageSize - the number of records per page (use 0 for no pagination)
      Returns:
      List of TimetableBean matching the search criteria
      Throws:
      ApplicationException - if any error occurs during the search operation
